fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Minnesota Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to minnesota, pharmacy technician compact minnesota, minnesota pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Minnesota Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Minnesota Board of Pharmacy
2829 University Ave. SE, Suite 530
Minneapolis, MN 55414-3251

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Minnesota.

steps.title: Create an Account on eLicense Minnesota
steps.description: Visit the Minnesota Board of Pharmacy's eLicense portal at https://mn.gov/boards/pharmacy/elicense/ and click 'New Applicants Create an Account' to register your profile. You will need to verify your email address to activate your account.

steps.title: Complete and Submit the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created eLicense account. From the dashboard, locate and select the 'New Pharmacy Technician Application'. Complete all required fields accurately, provide attestations, and submit the non-refundable application fee of $35 through the portal.

steps.title: Complete a Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Register for a fingerprint-based criminal background check through IdentoGO at www.identogo.com. Select Minnesota, enter service code 249Q9F, and schedule your fingerprinting appointment at an authorized location. Ensure the results are directed to the Minnesota Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log in to your eLicense Minnesota account to check the status of your application. The Board will review your submitted application, fee, and CBC results. Once approved, you will receive notification via email regarding your technician registration and can verify it on the Board's licensee search tool.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Minnesota pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Minnesota have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Minnesota. To work in Minnesota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Minnesota
hero.dek: Apply online through the Minnesota Board of Pharmacy's eLicense portal (mn.gov/boards/pharmacy/elicense/). Create an account, select the 'New Pharmacy Technician Application', and follow the instructions, including the required criminal background check.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the eLicense portal and click 'New Applicants Create an Account' to register. Once registered, log in and search for the 'Pharmacy Technician Application' to begin your application.
